message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>As COVID-19 vaccination begins worldwide, policymakers face critical trade-offs. Using a mathematical model of COVID-19 transmission, we find that timing of the rollout is expected to have a substantially greater impact on mortality than risk-based prioritization and uptake and that prioritizing first doses over second doses may be life saving.</jats:p>
title: The impacts of COVID-19 vaccine timing, number of doses, and risk prioritization on mortality in the US
